name: Oxygen partial pressure in arterial blood

concept.conceptName: Oxygen [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Arterial blood

en.name: Oxygen partial pressure in arterial blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context The arterial partial pressure of oxygen (PaO2) is the pressure exerted by O2 dissolved in arterial blood. It reflects the efficiency of pulmonary gas exchange and the alveolar-arterial transfer of oxygen. PaO2 falls in respiratory failure, shunt, ventilation/perfusion mismatch, and diffusion impairment. Together with the inspired oxygen fraction (FiO2), it forms the PaO2/FiO2 ratio used to grade hypoxaemia severity (ARDS Berlin criteria) [1, 2]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a blood gas analyser on an arterial sample. Results are reported as a partial pressure in mmHg (US convention) or kPa (SI / European convention) [3]. **Note: arterial vs venous.** Arterial PO2 (~80-100 mmHg under room air in healthy adults) is much higher than venous PO2 (~40 mmHg). They are fundamentally different measurands and must not be merged. This concept set covers **arterial** PO2 only. Venous, mixed venous and central venous PO2 are captured by the separate concept set "Oxygen partial pressure in venous blood". **Note: temperature-corrected variant.** Modern blood gas analysers measure at 37 °C. A separate LOINC code exists for PO2 corrected to the patient's actual temperature (relevant in hypothermia). Both forms are included here; downstream analyses that need to distinguish them must use the LOINC code, not the value. ## Included Concepts The set lists two LOINC "Lab Test" concepts for PO2 in arterial blood (cord blood excluded): - *3027801 / 2703-7 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: most widely used. - *3022803 / 19255-9 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: temperature-corrected variant. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mm[Hg]`** (concept 8876 — millimetre of mercury). Conversion: `1 mmHg = 0.1333 kPa`, `1 kPa ≈ 7.50 mmHg`. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concepts that exist for O2 in arterial blood are deliberately not part of this set, grouped by reason for exclusion. ### P50 — different measurand - *3015968 / 19214-6 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] saturation adjusted to 0.5 in Arterial blood*: this is P50, the PO2 at which haemoglobin is 50% saturated — a marker of haemoglobin oxygen affinity, not the routine PaO2. ### Cord blood specimens — physiologically distinct - *3021706 / 28648-4 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] in Arterial cord blood* - *3033254 / 49696-8 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Arterial cord blood* ### Oxygen saturation — different measurand - All `Oxygen saturation` codes: covered by the separate "Oxygen saturation in arterial blood" concept set. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports an arterial PO2 (e.g. blood gas analyser): `3027801 / 2703-7 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] in Arterial blood`. - **Source unit is kPa**: convert to mmHg using `1 kPa ≈ 7.50 mmHg`. - **Source reports PO2 from venous, mixed venous or central venous blood**: do **not** map to this concept set. Use "Oxygen partial pressure in venous blood" instead. - **Source reports oxygen saturation (SaO2/SpO2/%)**: do **not** map. Use "Oxygen saturation in arterial blood" (different measurand). - **Source reports P50** (haemoglobin oxygen affinity): out of scope of this concept set. - **Do not confuse with**: venous PO2 (separate concept set), oxygen saturation (separate concept set), inspired oxygen fraction (FiO2, ventilator setting). ## References [1] ARDS Definition Task Force, Ranieri VM, Rubenfeld GD, Thompson BT, Ferguson ND, Caldwell E, et al. Acute respiratory distress syndrome: the Berlin Definition. *JAMA*. 2012;307(23):2526-33. Available: https://doi.org/10.1001/jama.2012.5669 [2] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2703-7 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] in Arterial blood. Available: https://loinc.org/2703-7/ [3] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
